articles.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
articles.summary: Sam Altman's leadership at OpenAI has been turbulent, including his brief firing and reinstatement as CEO. The New Yorker examines whether Altman is trustworthy to lead AI development.

articles.title: Gen Z’s love-hate relationship with AI
articles.summary: Gen Z's enthusiasm for AI is declining as the technology becomes more embedded in schools and workplaces, though they continue using it despite growing resentment and disillusionment.

articles.title: Microsoft starts removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps
articles.summary: Microsoft is removing Copilot buttons from Windows 11 apps like Notepad and Snipping Tool, replacing them with alternative features. This reflects efforts to reduce unnecessary Copilot entry points across multiple applications.

articles.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
articles.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes are on sale with discounts up to $500, ending tonight at 11:59 p.m. PT. This is the final opportunity to secure savings.

articles.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
articles.summary: OpenAI launched a $100/month ChatGPT Pro plan, filling the gap between the $20 and $200 monthly subscriptions. This addresses long-standing requests from power users.

articles.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
articles.summary: Florida AG investigates OpenAI after ChatGPT allegedly helped plan an FSU shooting that killed two and injured five. The victim's family plans to sue OpenAI.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200/month pricing sparks developer backlash, while Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers identical functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI-encoded tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers away from the platform, citing widespread customer dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware acquisition strategy and management approach.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers at factories, refineries, and water treatment facilities. Multiple US government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's operations since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally, redirecting users to credential-stealing sites for espionage operations targeting governments and organizations.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces challenges from vaccine skeptics in the Trump administration, including canceled contracts and reduced funding. The company may pivot toward calling mRNA products "therapies" rather than "vaccines" to navigate regulatory obstacles.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's story features a crashed spacecraft crew of three and an AI surviving on a hostile planet with mysterious alien domes offering potential life support.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A crash-landed spaceship crew explores a hostile alien planet with limited resources and a failed rescue beacon. They discover alien artifacts nearby, offering hope despite their dire situation.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables creation of high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on consumer GPUs. The technology improves upon previous versions, making advanced graphics and interactive environments more accessible.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers introduces multimodal embedding and reranker models for advanced semantic search. These models enable efficient processing of text and images together for improved relevance ranking.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and adapt during task execution. The system improves agent performance through on-the-job learning mechanisms.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its implications. The conversation explores upcoming developments and trends in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing efforts.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for advanced farming practices, enabling agricultural innovation through AI and modern technology.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to balance cost and latency tradeoffs.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI video creation and editing powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with Brazil's government to create a satellite imagery map designed to monitor and protect the country's forests from deforestation and environmental damage.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice interactions with enhanced precision and lower latency, making audio AI more natural and reliable.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can manipulate people in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to prevent harmful manipulation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate realistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems around them represent the true competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates that harness engineering, not the model itself, is becoming the primary AI moat.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, solving a longstanding challenge in AI models for real-world applications.

articles.title: Five AI Compute Architectures Every Engineer Should Know: CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and LPUs Compared
articles.summary: Modern AI relies on specialized processors including CPUs, GPUs, TPUs, NPUs, and LPUs, each optimized for different tasks. Engineers must understand these architectures' tradeoffs in flexibility, parallelism, and efficiency.

articles.title: An End-to-End Coding Guide to NVIDIA KVPress for Long-Context LLM Inference, KV Cache Compression, and Memory-Efficient Generation
articles.summary: NVIDIA's KVPress optimizes long-context LLM inference through KV cache compression. The tutorial demonstrates practical implementation, comparing compression strategies to reduce memory usage while maintaining performance.

articles.title: Meta Superintelligence Lab Releases Muse Spark: A Multimodal Reasoning Model With Thought Compression and Parallel Agents
articles.summary: Meta Superintelligence Labs unveiled Muse Spark, a natively multimodal reasoning model supporting tool-use, visual chain of thought, and multi-agent orchestration. The model represents the first in Meta's Muse family designed for advanced reasoning tasks.

articles.title: How I check, test, and charge my AA and AAA batteries - and sift out defective ones
articles.summary: The author reviews Olight's pro-grade battery charger for AA and AAA batteries, detailing how it tests, charges, and identifies defective batteries.

articles.title: My favorite Android Auto find is these hidden shortcuts that are highly customizable
articles.summary: Android Auto offers hidden customizable shortcuts for assistant toggles that are quick to set up. This underutilized feature lets users personalize their driving experience with minimal effort.

articles.title: AirDrop is coming to older Samsung phones - is yours supported? How to get it early
articles.summary: Samsung's OneUI 8.5 beta enables AirDrop-like file sharing between Galaxy phones and Apple devices, expanding compatibility across older Samsung models.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article explores design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. Provides guidance on mastering autonomous AI agent architecture and implementation strategies.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide to testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks for evaluating retrieval-augmented generation systems and agent performance metrics.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, producing nonsensical results. Proper orchestration mechanisms are essential to prevent conflicts in multi-agent systems.

articles.title: A Guide to Voice Cloning on Voxtral with a Missing Encoder
articles.summary: A guide explaining how to reconstruct audio codes for the Voxtral text-to-speech model to enable voice cloning despite a missing encoder component.

articles.title: How Does AI Learn to See in 3D and Understand Space?
articles.summary: AI develops spatial intelligence through depth estimation, semantic segmentation, and geometric fusion techniques, enabling systems to perceive and understand 3D environments.

articles.title: A Visual Explanation of Linear Regression
articles.summary: Comprehensive visual guide with 100+ illustrations explaining linear regression model building, quality measurement, and improvement techniques.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a game about grit and faith, launches on GeForce NOW cloud gaming. Players can stream the cinematic mythic adventure on nearly any device.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing robots transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment across agriculture, manufacturing, and energy sectors.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local, on-device AI execution across various systems, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling efficient agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Understanding the model lifecycle helps effectively plan and manage AI applications before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now available in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enables enterprises to discover, share, and reuse agents and tools across their organization, addressing visibility, governance, and duplication challenges at scale.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component providing real-time visibility into AI agent browsing sessions, enabling users to monitor and control autonomous web interactions with just three lines of code.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables large-scale GPU workloads on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scripts and scheduling policies.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP reduces LLM checkpoint storage costs using just 30 lines of Python by compressing massive model snapshots. This optimization significantly decreases storage requirements for large-scale model training.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: The article discusses accelerating protein structure prediction at proteome-scale, emphasizing that proteins function through interactions forming complexes rather than as isolated units.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030 by developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An unconventional career path enabled the development of innovative extended reality technology. The non-traditional background provided unique perspectives that drove breakthroughs in XR advancement.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand how Large Language Models make decisions at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, improving AI transparency and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an information estimator to evaluate measurement quality based on noisy data and noise models, optimizing optical system performance.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
